purpose: Audit the planning structure, build structure, notes, reports, audits, commit history, and build progression of vbtprobuild crack-repair Waves 1-11 so the repo-agnostic workflow can persist the exact governance logic without copying chat prompts as authority.

source_refs.final_wave_1_11_closeout_visibility_comm: f06d07fd Refresh indexes after closeout commit

core_conclusion.summary: Waves 1-11 were not a prompt-driven build. They were a repo-native governance loop: promote one active authority plan, save only actionable preimplementation notes, implement a named owner bundle, prove it with focused validators/tests, commit implementation separately from generated discovery refreshes, and close out with checkpoint/receipt proof only at real gates.
core_conclusion.most_important_pattern: Codex reasoning was retained by writing decisions into durable authority artifacts at the moment they affected future work, while evidence-only reports and generated indexes stayed queryable but non-authoritative.
core_conclusion.notable_negative_rule: Broad reports, index freshness, stale scanner output, handoff notes, and head-only generated-index drift were not treated as behavioral proof or new work by themselves.

single_active_plan_authority.pattern: The crack-repair execution plan became the running ledger for the entire wave chain.
evidence: 6d8f2312 promoted the crack repair plan and supersession register.
The plan_note_active_set contained one active plan after closeout.
The old overlay plan was classified historical_non_authoritative and superseded by the crack repair plan.
single_active_plan_authority.current_decision_after_wave_11: Waves 1-11 and the post-Wave-11 closeout checks completed. The plan remains the current closeout authority so future work does not accidentally revive superseded blocker semantics.
single_active_plan_authority.next_action_after_wave_11: No repair implementation wave remains open; future work starts from a new tracked follow-up only if a new root problem is discovered.

prep_note_commits.pattern: Prep notes were committed only when they narrowed ambiguity or changed future wave order, owners, validators, full-read needs, safety boundaries, index requirements, or activation gates.
commits: e5ec079d Record Wave 3 overlay taxonomy plan
f0463545 Record Wave 6 VBT642 mode plan
a31f2b97 Record future wave repair details
prep_note_commits.why_it_worked: The notes were compact and attached to the active plan. They prevented broad replanning while still preserving Codex's reasoning for later waves.

plan_note_projection.pattern: manifests/v2/plan_note_compiled.json was refreshed when active plan notes changed, making the reasoning discoverable without treating every report as authority.
plan_note_projection.risk_to_port: The projection is large and noisy in diffs. A repo-agnostic port should keep the same authority semantics but prefer compact queryable indexes where possible.

report_and_audit_role.pattern: Tmp reports and audits were promoted as durable evidence, indexed by output schema and plan-note indexes, but most were not active authority.
report_and_audit_role.authority_boundary: The active plan/checkpoint/receipt controlled next action; reports explained why but did not override the plan.

stale_output_classification.pattern: Generated output, scanner, exact-path, handoff, and superseded-plan findings were evidence unless paired with a required validator failure, focused test failure, or hard safety invariant failure.
stale_output_classification.closeout_effect: No Wave 12 was opened for evidence-only findings.

common_surfaces: contract JSON when cross-surface semantics needed it
thin src module or helper only when runtime/domain behavior was owned
owner_bundle_shape.implementation_rule: Each implementation commit owned one coherent bundle and avoided mixing unrelated future placeholders into the slice.
owner_bundle_shape.proof_rule: Focused validators and focused tests were behavioral proof. Index freshness was proof only for waves that owned generated discovery or closeout gates.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: private full-real activation config, schema, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Direct implementation was acceptable because the authority plan already had enough detail.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: source acquisition policy, source registry, live promotion policy, production pull rules, src helpers, operational materialization runner, focused no-network/live-source tests
per_wave_build_progression.logic: This was broader than later waves but still coherent because the source-readiness boundary was explicit.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: overlay policy, schema, contract, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: The prep note defined hard_failure, research_gap, warning, and pass classifications before coding.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: bridge policy, schema, contract, thin data bridge module, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: The bridge stayed thin and avoided provider execution, portfolio construction, and production promotion claims.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: research mode policy, schema, contract, src helper, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Research, acquisition, promotion, portfolio, and optimizer statuses remained separate.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: mode-aware matrix policy, schema, contract, src helper, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Catalog-only records stopped being universal no-runtime blockers; mode fields carried the exact allowed state.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: boundary helper policy, schema, contract, src helper, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Repeated blocker semantics moved into one helper instead of being duplicated across validators.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: helper reference reconciliation, helper/script/repo/config/output index refreshes, checkpoint and index receipts, worktree-noise fixture updates
per_wave_build_progression.logic: This wave owned discovery reconciliation, so generated refresh outputs were part of the implementation bundle instead of incidental noise.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: MCP runtime precedence policy, schema, contract, validator, focused test
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Private docs/source/runtime precedence was made explicit without persisting private URLs or secrets.

per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: read-only harness policy, schema, contract, validator, harness script, tests, script surface usage policy update
per_wave_build_progression.logic: A discovery refresh happened before implementation because Wave 9 surfaces had to be visible for read-only/no-write cleanup.

per_wave_build_progression.role: Legacy scanner and exact-path authority cleanup
per_wave_build_progression.owner_shape: scanner exact-path policy, schema, contract, validator, tests, script surface policy updates, closeout checkpoint and receipt
per_wave_build_progression.logic: Stale index misses and legacy scanner findings became evidence-only unless a real validator/test/safety failure existed.

a69d2cea Implement private VBT Data bridge wave

generated_refresh_commits: e0fd13d9 Refresh indexes for post Wave 11 closeout
f06d07fd Refresh indexes after closeout commit
commit_history_pattern.observed_commit_boundary_rule: Plan-note prep, implementation, generated refresh, and closeout proof were usually separate commits. Wave 8 is the intentional exception because its owner behavior was generated discovery reconciliation.

refresh_triggers_that_were_used: active plan authority or plan-note projection changed
the selected wave owned helper/index/generated discovery reconciliation
future wave discovery depended on newly added surfaces
a closeout gate required fresh discovery before final validation
new closeout checkpoint/receipt artifacts needed to become discoverable
refresh_triggers_rejected: head-only generated-index staleness after an index commit
scanner or exact-path evidence with no validator/test/safety failure

repeatable_governance_loop.name: select_or_create_authority
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Use one active roadmap/plan/packet as next-action authority and classify older notes as implemented, superseded, draft, historical, or evidence-only.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: readiness_question
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Before implementation, decide whether the slice is ready or whether a compact prep note is needed to preserve future-affecting reasoning.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: targeted_prep_note_when_needed
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Persist only actionable discoveries about order, owners, validators, full reads, safety boundaries, index requirements, activation gates, or closeout criteria.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: owner_bundle_implementation
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Implement only the packet or plan owner bundle: policy/config, schema, contract when useful, helper/module when owned, validator, and focused tests.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: focused_proof
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Run JSON parse checks, owning validator, focused tests, relevant read-only/worktree checks, and diff checks. Do not use broad index status as behavioral proof.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: implementation_commit
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Commit the coherent owner bundle after proof, with exact intended paths.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: conditional_generated_refresh
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Refresh generated discovery outputs only for explicit ownership, stale discovery blockers, future-wave dependency, closeout gates, or operator request.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: generated_refresh_commit_once
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: If generated outputs are refreshed, batch the required surfaces from the same implementation head and commit them once.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: no_head_chasing
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: After a generated-index commit, do not refresh again merely because the index now describes its parent commit.

repeatable_governance_loop.name: closeout_gate_only_when_real
repeatable_governance_loop.rule: Write checkpoint/receipt artifacts only for writers, generators, materializers, external actions, irreversible decisions, or real closeout gates.

persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.pattern: Git and GitHub tracking should be structural, not optional.
persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.port_rule: The workflow should define commit boundaries for planning prep, implementation, generated refresh, and closeout proof, and should make each slice packet name the expected commit plan.

Implement or require a plan-note/packet authority index with active_eligible, authority_class, supersedes, superseded_by, current_decision, next_action, validation refs, and protected surface impacts.

persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.pattern: Evidence must stay distinct from authority.
persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.port_rule: Reports, audits, copied external evidence, receipts, checkpoints, manifests, and generated indexes need an artifact/evidence index with source path, checksum, writer/generator, secret-safe status, and authority boundary.
A slice is not ready until it names the owning validator or the exact validator extension. Broad status commands are navigation evidence only.

persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.pattern: Runtime-affecting values belong outside hidden code.
persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.port_rule: Packets should force config, policy, contract, schema, or explicit operator selection for runtime values.

persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.pattern: Generated refresh needs a decision object.
persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.port_rule: Slice packets should carry a structured refresh_decision naming whether refresh is skipped or required, why, exact surfaces, commands, validation, and commit boundary.

A repo-agnostic receipt/checkpoint validator should reject status-only receipts and allow receipts tied to writers, generators, materializers, external actions, irreversible decisions, or closeout gates.

persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.pattern: Worktree noise is a policy surface.
persistence_patterns_to_port_into_repo_a.port_rule: Untracked reports, generated artifacts, stale indexes, and external evidence should be classified by policy so they do not silently become blockers or authority.

porting_cautions: Do not copy vbtprobuild's domain-specific VBT policies into repo-agnostic doctrine; copy the governance roles and proof boundaries.
Do not turn prep notes into mandatory bureaucracy. The observed rule was conditional: write a prep note only when it preserves actionable future reasoning.
Do not make every wave write receipts. The successful pattern avoided status-confetti artifacts.
Do not make generated indexes a universal proof gate. They were routing evidence except when the selected wave or closeout gate owned generated discovery.
Do not rely on chat memory or prompt text as durable reasoning. Persist decisions in plan, packet, register, contract, validator, report, receipt, or checkpoint artifacts.
audit_result: The vbtprobuild Wave 1-11 build progression is a strong source model for repo-agnostic build governance. The transferable core is the authority/evidence separation plus commit-boundary discipline: plan or packet controls the slice, focused validators/tests prove behavior, generated refreshes are conditional, and closeout artifacts are written only when they prove a real gate.
